model_phrasing: Model phrasing: GPT-5.x detected from the system harness; Phrasing profile id RTM-PH6OCQ6MX4.
decision_target: Choose signal-only structured lifetime with an exact cancellation-classification rule versus adding another cancellation surface.
source_of_truth: The executable DKR contract, v3 cancellation probe and saved result, current Lite source, the v2 checkpoint, and the rejecting validator record that triggered this revision.
read_method: From the repository root run bash .okra/runs/issue-triage-session-20260715/workers/dkr-2-v3/replay.sh to pin all hashes, replay 16 behavior and API cases, compare the saved contract, and validate this checkpoint.
questions_answered: The root replay command is repository-root-resolvable and does not depend on executable mode.
Scalar child, scalar error, stream completion, producer error, consumer break, stream parent close, and three-level nested close replay.
Targeted caller cancellation and sibling contexts remain isolated from their parent and siblings.
An uncooperative scalar makes close wait and defers parent resource cleanup; bounded timeout remains adapter policy.
An exit is cancellation only when the effective signal is aborted and the error is signal.reason by identity or has name AbortError.
A different original error thrown after abort remains the invocation error by identity and is not classified as cancellation.
Close order is abort descendants, return active stream iterators, join descendants, classify without replacing errors, then clean parent resources.
The owner, invocation caller, and running flow all have control through close, optional caller signal, and ExecutionContext.signal; public cancel adds no caller.
No public cancel, start, spawn, task handle, worker pool, session semantic, or public task registry is required.
questions_unanswered: The private direct-descendant and active-iterator bookkeeping still needs product implementation review.
Independent validation has not replayed v3 behavior, hashes, wall reads, or the classification rule.
decision: candidate only: add ExecutionContext.signal and optional signal on exec and execStream; close aborts descendants, returns active stream iterators, joins descendants, classifies an exit as aborted only when the effective signal is aborted and the error is signal.reason by identity or error.name is AbortError, preserves every unrelated original error, then cleans parent resources; do not add ExecutionContext.cancel, start, spawn, task handles, worker pools, or session semantics
flag_if_missing_or_stale: cannot: fail closed and rerun DKR-2 v3 when Lite source, a probe or contract hash, freshness, replay result, or independent review changes

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: V3 generic scalar, stream, nested, signal classification, isolation, and uncooperative probes
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Replay 16 cases and confirm the candidate adds no session, cancel, start, spawn, worker-pool, or task surface.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Read-only baseline replay and pinned unchanged Lite source hashes
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Replay existing scalar and stream baselines and verify both current Lite source hashes without product changes.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: V3 writer checkpoint without a separate successful validator replay
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Keep the value at one until a separate validator replays every claim and wall.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Authorized v3 worker paths, content hashes, root replay, governed progress, and run-store verification
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Resolve all eight hashes, compare writes with the packet allowlist, append through the absolute helper, and verify the run store.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: V3 checkpoint with numeric walls, breached independence, candidate wording, and blocked downstream gate
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Validate identity, numeric walls, distinct refs, classification rule, restricted surface, and blocked downstream advance.

wall_gate.reason: V3 is writer-only evidence; single_llm_truth_acceptance_count remains one until a separate validator succeeds.

worked_checkpoint_trace.decision: rejected for downstream advance pending independent replay

confidence_probability_update: 0.91 after v2 probes; 0.94 after root replay repair and explicit identity, AbortError-name, unrelated-error, sibling-context, and uncooperative-scalar probes
risk_or_anti_goal_implications: Cancellation classification never replaces the invocation error and does not hide an unrelated post-abort failure.
An AbortError is cancellation only after the effective signal has aborted.
Uncooperative work can delay close indefinitely; adapters own timeout policy.
The candidate remains generic Lite lifecycle behavior with private bookkeeping.

candidate_pkrs.scope: Implement the accepted signal-only context and ordered close semantics with private bookkeeping.
Add generic classification, scalar, stream, nested, caller, isolation, ordering, and uncooperative contract tests.

checkpoint_rejection_policy: Superficial, tautological, false-positive, fabricated, unsupported, or single-LLM-truth checkpoints are rejected. Missing, stale, wrong-source, non-replayable, or contradicted evidence fails closed. An empty result remains valid only with a replayable probe trace.
role_grammar: CKRs are measurable contribution context, not worker work.
DKRs are discovery-worker scopes; PKRs are progression-worker execution units; there is no CKR worker.
